notes:
Plaintiff opening work notes for arb-ex10-20260630103350.

Objective: prove by preponderance that before Jan. 1, 2027 00:00 PST, Polymarket launched an official fungible token, excluding NFTs, promotions, points, or collaboration/community launches.

Opportunity: openings:plaintiff. Allowed final act: record_opening_statement only. Evidence admission not allowed.

Record inspection: get_case/list_evidence show only five initial case-packet files, all case_packet and juror-visible. Read full contents of ev_9f790b70e7f8_market-page, ev_2c067be8800a_primary-evidence, ev_ce624e6c59c1_official-source-record, ev_38c7491e77a8_unresolved-record-gaps, ev_8676b8beeed6_secondary-reporting.

Evidence analysis:
- market-page preserves proposition, elements, and deadline but no real Polymarket market URL/id.
- primary-evidence summarizes official Polymarket docs: pUSD described as Polymarket collateral token for all trading, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, USDC-backed, transferable, 6 decimals; official contracts page lists pUSD / CollateralToken proxy at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB; changelog/V2 migration say pUSD launched with CLOB V2 production around Apr. 28, 2026.
- official-source-record gives source map and quotes from docs.polymarket.com pages plus Polygonscan corroboration. Strong for official-channel attribution, but captures were post-run and lack server timestamps/archive proof.
- unresolved-record-gaps candidly identifies missing market URL/id, lack of pre-run market/API capture, no immutable archive/server date headers, and defense semantic theory: pUSD may be characterized as technical collateral introduced with CLOB V2 rather than an official token launch.
- secondary-reporting says no secondary reporting captured.

Native investigation: searched official Polymarket docs for pUSD/CLOB V2/contracts/address and live docs appeared consistent with packet leads. Not admitted evidence during opening; preserve only as source targets for later phases. Later admission targets: official docs pages /concepts/pusd, /resources/contracts, /changelog, /v2-migration; Polygonscan address page; archive/server-dated captures if available. Adverse checks later: search Polymarket statements or docs that distinguish pUSD from a public token launch or characterize it as purely internal/non-public collateral.

Theory: pUSD is the qualifying token because it is fungible, official, publicly communicated by Polymarket, and live before the deadline. The main dispute is legal/semantic: whether an official ERC-20 collateral token for all trading counts under the proposition. Missing provenance details affect weight but not opening theory; later phases should submit direct source captures before relying on external material.

Opening opportunity openings:defendant. Inspected get_case/list_evidence: no new submitted evidence beyond initial five case-packet files, plus plaintiff opening. Stat/read performed for all five files. Packet analysis: ev_9f790b70e7f8_market-page is preserved condition text only; no market URL/identifier and no broader factual research. ev_2c067be8800a_primary-evidence summarizes official Polymarket docs and Polygonscan captures after a prior run; helpful to plaintiff but derivative. ev_ce624e6c59c1_official-source-record maps docs URLs and extracted text; it expressly notes post-run captures and missing server publication timestamps/archive snapshots. ev_38c7491e77a8_unresolved-record-gaps is key defense support: missing real market URL/identifier, missing pre-run market/API capture, post-run official docs preservation, missing server-dated headers/archive/publication histories, and semantic issue distinguishing official token launch from technical collateral token in CLOB V2. ev_8676b8beeed6_secondary-reporting says no secondary reporting captured and no preliminary factual research performed. Native investigation for opening theory only: searched web for official Polymarket pUSD docs/changelog and adverse token-launch framing. Search surfaced official docs pages already named by packet and adverse/limiting snippets stating pUSD is collateral and no official tradable governance token/airdrop had launched; not admitted and will not be cited as evidence. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source map and unresolved gaps. Planned defense opening: burden remains on plaintiff; current record may show a Polymarket-documented ERC-20 collateral mechanism but has not yet shown a qualifying official fungible token launch under the condition. Later proof should capture direct official pages with dates, archive/server metadata, official social/blog announcements if any, on-chain deployment/admin/custody, and adverse official/secondary material distinguishing pUSD from platform/governance token launches.

Plaintiff merits work notes for arb-ex10-20260630103350.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Allowed: submit_evidence and submit_argument. Defendant opening argued pUSD is merely collateral infrastructure and highlighted provenance gaps.

Record re-scan: get_case/list_evidence before merits showed only original case-packet files and both openings; no pre-existing submitted evidence. Exact contents of original packet were read during opening and reviewed from notes.

Evidence/source plan: decisive elements are fungibility, official release by Polymarket, public communication through official Polymarket channels, timing before Jan. 1, 2027, and exclusions. Source targets: official docs pUSD page, contracts page, changelog, V2 migration, on-chain explorer for listed address, and adverse/clarifying official source on whether Polymarket 'has a token.'

Native investigation and submitted evidence:
1. curl -L https://docs.polymarket.com/concepts/pusd.md. Headers: HTTP/2 200, text/markdown, date Tue 30 Jun 2026 11:12:19 GMT, Vercel/Mintlify markdown matched path. Submitted as ev_b5b4cf7d908f_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-b5b4cf7d908f. Shows pUSD = Polymarket USD, collateral token used for all trading, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, backed by USDC, transferable, 6 decimals, designed for Polymarket.
2. curl -L https://docs.polymarket.com/resources/contracts.md. Submitted as ev_838f5b652691_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-838f5b652691. Shows official contracts page, single source of truth for platform contract addresses, lists pUSD CollateralToken proxy 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB and implementation/onramp/offramp.
3. curl -L https://docs.polymarket.com/changelog.md. Submitted as ev_9afebe9fe2ad_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-9afebe9fe2ad. Shows Apr. 17 2026 coordinated upgrade including new collateral token pUSD and Apr. 28 2026 CLOB V2 live on production.
4. curl -L https://docs.polymarket.com/v2-migration.md. Submitted as ev_698e8127dde8_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-698e8127dde8. Shows CLOB V2 live as of Apr. 28 2026, new contracts/backend/collateral token pUSD, standard ERC-20 backed by USDC.
5. curl -L Polygonscan address page for official pUSD address. Submitted as ev_e447a6447df7_submitted-evidence-05-plaintiff-e447a6447df7. Shows title Polymarket: pUSD Token, verified/token metadata and transaction count. Corroborative; not official Polymarket source.
6. Adverse/clarifying search found official Help Center 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?' Fetched with curl and extracted article text from HTML/__NEXT_DATA__. Submitted as ev_b3b269de98e4_submitted-evidence-06-plaintiff-b3b269de98e4. Shows all trading and liquidity rewards are in pUSD, Polymarket's official collateral token; pUSD standard ERC-20 backed by USDC; Polymarket has not announced airdrop or token generation event. This directly addresses defense: pUSD is official, while not a TGE/airdrop.

Adverse checks: Search found secondary reporting saying Polymarket upgraded protocol/launched pUSD, but I did not submit secondary reports because official sources are stronger and side has limited evidence slots. Main adverse fact is official Help Center's no airdrop/TGE statement; submitted it because it clarifies, rather than defeats, plaintiff theory.

Reasoning: Record now proves the proposition by preponderance if 'official fungible token' is read by its stated elements. pUSD is fungible ERC-20, officially documented, publicly communicated, deployed/listed by Polymarket for all trading, and live in production by Apr. 28, 2026. It is not NFT, promotion, points, or collaboration/community token. Defense's 'ordinary token launch' theory adds requirements (airdrop/TGE/speculative governance token) absent from the proposition and partly contradicted by official Help Center calling pUSD official collateral token while separately warning no airdrop/TGE.

Remaining limitations: No immutable April archive snapshots or server publication history. Docs are current captures with official content and dated changelog entries. Polygonscan corroborates on-chain public existence but not official attribution; attribution rests on docs/help pages.

Merits opportunity arguments:defendant. Inspected current record/list: plaintiff added six submitted evidence items: pUSD docs, contracts page, changelog, V2 migration, Polygonscan, Help Center article. Stat/read performed for key plaintiff items and exact contents reviewed. Plaintiff PX-1 is strong for pUSD as ERC-20, transferable, backed by USDC, used for all trading, but also states day-to-day user experience does not change, pUSD is the technical settlement layer, represents a USDC claim, and has no current external exchange listing plans. PX-6 Help Center is critical: official Help Center article headline asks 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?', states all trading/liquidity rewards are in pUSD as official collateral token, but separately states Polymarket has not announced plans for any airdrop or token generation event and to beware of scams. PX-3/PX-4 frame pUSD as CLOB V2 migration: new exchange contracts, rewritten backend, migration from USDC.e to pUSD, normal users handled by frontend. Polygonscan is corroborative but not official attribution. Native investigation: searched web for Polymarket token/airdrop and exact CMO quote, opened CoinDesk article 'Polymarket Will Launch Token and Airdrop After U.S. Relaunch, CMO Says' (Oct. 24, 2025). Submitted secondary extract as defense evidence ev_2b6994532a43_submitted-evidence-07-defendant-2b6994532a43. Weight limited because secondary and not official, but useful context that market discourse and reported CMO comments treated 'a token' and 'airdrop' as future after U.S. relaunch, distinct from pUSD collateral. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Defense argument will concede pUSD official collateral/fungible/before deadline, but argue plaintiff overreads 'official fungible token launch'; official evidence describes pUSD as technical settlement/infrastructure collateral, while Help Center and secondary context distinguish it from a TGE/airdrop/token launch in ordinary market sense.

Plaintiff rebuttal work notes for arb-ex10-20260630103350.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Allowed tools include submit_evidence and submit_rebuttal/pass. Remaining evidence slots are limited, so I investigated defendant's new exhibit and decided no new evidence is needed.

Record/evidence scan: prompt and list show defendant merits plus new evidence ev_2b6994532a43_submitted-evidence-07-defendant-2b6994532a43. Used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range for exact content. Metadata: CoinDesk secondary report, submitted by defendant in arguments; source URL https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2025/10/24/polymarket-will-launch-token-and-airdrop-after-u-s-relaunch-cmo-says; relevance itself says weight is limited because secondary reporting, not official docs.

Exact-content analysis of D-X1: It reports headline 'Polymarket Will Launch Token and Airdrop After U.S. Relaunch, CMO Says'; subheadline/summary says there will be a token and airdrop after formal U.S. relaunch. It quotes the CMO saying 'There will be a token, there will be an airdrop' and later focus on the token after U.S. app/launch. It says no token mechanics or use cases were provided. Its own analysis note says it is secondary reporting, not official Polymarket source or direct podcast media, and is probative only for context.

Rebuttal reasoning: D-X1 can prove only that another future airdrop/TGE-style token was discussed in October 2025 reporting. It does not disprove that pUSD was official, fungible, public, and launched by Apr. 28, 2026. The proposition does not require first/only token, airdrop, TGE, governance, external listing, or speculative public-token event. PX-6 actually distinguishes the concepts: no announced airdrop/TGE, but all trading/rewards are in pUSD, Polymarket's official collateral token. That helps plaintiff because the market exclusions do not remove collateral tokens.

Plan: file concise rebuttal addressing defense's strongest semantic argument and D-X1. Offer only a few relevant existing exhibits if allowed/needed: PX-6 Help Center, PX-1 pUSD page, PX-3 or PX-4 timing. No new evidence or technical report needed.

Surrebuttal opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal submitted no new source files; it re-offered existing evidence as PX-R1 Help Center, PX-R2 pUSD page, PX-R3 defendant CoinDesk. Rebuttal's strongest points: market text does not require first/only/governance/exchange-listed/airdrop/TGE token; pUSD can be both infrastructure and a token; D-X1 is secondary and future token can coexist with pUSD. No new outside source needed because responsive points are interpretive and answered by admitted evidence. Surrebuttal plan: accept coexistence possibility but argue plaintiff still must prove the event described by the proposition. Use official PX-R1/PX-R2 to show Polymarket framed pUSD as official collateral/technical settlement wrapper, while article title and no airdrop/TGE language reflect a separate ordinary user token question. Emphasize 'official collateral token' is narrower than 'launched an official fungible token' and the condition is event-focused, not mere existence of any official ERC-20 in infrastructure. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing work notes for arb-ex10-20260630103350.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Only deliver_closing_statement allowed; no evidence admission. Performed required get_case and list_evidence final scan.

Final record posture: Plaintiff has official-source exhibits PX-1 pUSD docs, PX-2 Contracts page, PX-3 Changelog, PX-4 V2 Migration, PX-5 Polygonscan corroboration, PX-6 Help Center. Defendant submitted D-X1/PX-R3/DX-S3 CoinDesk secondary report about future token/airdrop after U.S. relaunch. Defendant surrebuttal repeats that pUSD is technical collateral and that Help Center distinguishes pUSD from a future airdrop/TGE.

Key concessions: Defendant's merits argument expressly does not dispute that pUSD exists, is an ERC-20, is documented by Polymarket, and went into production before Jan. 1, 2027.

Decisive proof:
- PX-1: pUSD is Polymarket USD, collateral token used for all trading, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, backed by USDC, transferable, 6 decimals.
- PX-2: official Polymarket Contracts page says single source of truth and lists pUSD CollateralToken proxy address.
- PX-3/PX-4: Apr. 17/Apr. 28 2026 official changelog/migration guide tie new pUSD collateral token to CLOB V2 production go-live.
- PX-6: official Help Center says all trading and liquidity rewards are in pUSD, Polymarket's official collateral token; no airdrop/TGE announced. This clarifies pUSD is official, not that it is excluded.
- PX-5: on-chain corroboration for address/title, secondary for attribution.

Closing theory: The only remaining dispute is an extra limitation defense asks council to add: official token launch means airdrop/TGE/governance/public speculative token. The proposition did not say that. It asked for an official fungible token and listed exclusions; pUSD is not within any exclusion. A token can be infrastructure and still a token. D-X1 may show another future token, but it does not negate pUSD satisfying the proposition. Provenance gaps (no immutable April archive snapshots) reduce perfection but not preponderance given current official pages, dated entries, and defense concessions.

Closing opportunity closings:defendant. Performed get_case and list_evidence; no new evidence after surrebuttal, plaintiff closing only. Plaintiff closing argues defense adds unstated airdrop/TGE/governance/external-listing/non-infrastructure limitations and that pUSD satisfies literal elements. Closing plan: concede pUSD's token mechanics, official documentation, and timing; focus on burden and event wording. Plaintiff's evidence proves official collateral infrastructure, not the public launch event described by proposition. Key admitted sources remain PX-1 pUSD page (technical settlement layer, wrapper representing USDC claim, no current external listing plans) and PX-6 Help Center (official collateral token, separate no announced airdrop/TGE in article framed as 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?'). D-X1 remains limited secondary context. No new source search because closing must use existing record. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
